title: SOCI Part 6A Protected Information — lawful disclosure register (whitelist of authorised disclosures and recipients)
exclusions: Excludes ad-hoc disclosures explicitly authorised by Court order or subpoena; track those in `legal_compelled_disclosure_lookup` with the order reference. Excludes routine intra-entity disclosures within the controlling-entity group covered by the entity-wide privacy / information-management framework.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.52.25` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `soci_clause="SOCI-Part-6A-register"`. Every row pins (disclosure_id, classification, sender_id, recipient_id, recipient_organisation, lawful_basis, basis_artefact_url, approved_at, approved_by, register_status). Dashboard `SOCI — Lawful Disclosure Register` surfaces all authorised disclosures.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Continuously evidences the whitelist of lawful disclosures with a documented lawful basis (e.g. authorised under section 42, 43, 44, 45 of Part 6A). Marked 'supports' because it complements UC-22.52.24 by making the authorised-disclosure space explicit and auditable, so unauthorised disclosures detected by UC-22.52.24 are unambiguous.
compliance.controlObjective: Maintains a defensible register of authorised disclosures so detection of unauthorised disclosures is high-confidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.52.25` archived to `audit_evidence`, the `SOCI — Lawful Disclosure Register` dashboard, and per-record lawful-basis-artefact URLs (Court orders, ASIO requests, ASD requests, regulator requests).

controlTest.positiveScenario: Author a lawful-disclosure register entry with `lawful_basis="s42_authorised_recipient_Secretary"`. UC-22.52.25 MUST record `register_status="current"` and feed `protected_info_authorised_disclosure_lookup`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Author a lawful-disclosure register entry without lawful basis. UC-22.52.25 MUST flag `register_status="basis_missing"`.

dataSources: GRC module (`index=grc sourcetype="snow:lawful_disclosure"`). Lookup `lawful_disclosure_register_lookup` (disclosure_id, classification, sender_id, recipient_id, recipient_organisation, recipient_email_domain, lawful_basis (CSV), basis_artefact_url, approved_at, approved_by, sunset_at, register_status).
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup lawful_disclosure_register_lookup
| eval days_since_approved = if(isnotnull(approved_at), round((now() - approved_at) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval days_to_sunset = if(isnotnull(sunset_at), round((sunset_at - now()) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval register_status = case(
    isnull(lawful_basis) OR lawful_basis="", "basis_missing",
    isnull(basis_artefact_url) OR basis_artefact_url="", "artefact_missing",
    isnull(approved_by) OR isnull(approved_at), "unapproved",
    isnotnull(sunset_at) AND sunset_at < now(), "expired",
    isnotnull(sunset_at) AND days_to_sunset <= 30, "approaching_sunset",
    1=1, "current")
| table disclosure_id classification sender_id recipient_id recipient_organisation lawful_basis approved_at approved_by sunset_at days_to_sunset basis_artefact_url register_status
description: Maintains the register of lawful disclosures of Protected Information per Part 6A: every authorised disclosure carries a documented lawful basis, an artefact, an approver, and a sunset date.
value: Defending against on-disclosure offences (Part 6A) requires demonstrating both (a) the entity prevented unauthorised disclosures and (b) authorised disclosures were lawful. This UC produces and maintains (b); UC-22.52.24 detects (a). Together they form the Part 6A compliance core.
implementation: (1) Maintain `lawful_disclosure_register_lookup` from the GRC module. (2) Feed the recipient-domain whitelist downstream into `protected_info_authorised_disclosure_lookup` consumed by UC-22.52.24. (3) Schedule UC-22.52.25 daily; route `basis_missing`, `artefact_missing`, `unapproved`, `expired` to notable severity=high. (4) Archive every row.
visualization: (Panel 1) Register entries by lawful_basis. (Panel 2) Recipient organisation map. (Panel 3) Sunset aging. (Panel 4) Approver concentration.

references.title: CISC Protected Information guidance (Part 6A) — secrecy provisions
Office of the Australian Information Commissioner — disclosure-register record-keeping guidance

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Long-lived disclosures** — authorised disclosures to a regulator may not have a sunset. Suppression: tolerate `sunset_at=null` with documented `lawful_basis` and a 12-month review reminder.

2. **Court orders with confidentiality**: the artefact may be sealed; surface a confidentiality reference instead of the artefact URL.

3. **Cross-jurisdictional disclosure**: a disclosure to a foreign Government may also be authorised under separate treaty arrangement. Suppression: separate `treaty_disclosure_lookup`.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `soci_22_52_25_exceptions`; max grant 30 days with General Counsel sign-off.
mitreAttack: N/A (compliance-register workflow — measures Part 6A lawful disclosure register currency, not adversary detection)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- UC-22.52.24 deployed.
- ES, ServiceNow add-on.
- `index=grc`, `index=audit_evidence`.
- Lookup `lawful_disclosure_register_lookup` columns: `disclosure_id`, `classification`, `sender_id`, `recipient_id`, `recipient_organisation`, `recipient_email_domain`, `lawful_basis` (e.g. `s42_authorised_recipient_Secretary`, `s43_authorised_recipient_ASD`, `s44_authorised_recipient_court`, `s45_authorised_recipient_regulator`), `basis_artefact_url`, `approved_at`, `approved_by`, `sunset_at`, `notes`.
- Authorised approvers list owned by General Counsel.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Materialise the lookup daily from the GRC; new entries created by the General Counsel team upon each authorised disclosure.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Schedule daily:

```ini
[UC-22.52.25: SOCI Part 6A lawful disclosure register]
cron_schedule = 30 12 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.52.25,reg=SOCI,clause=SOCI-Part-6A-register
action.notable.param.rule_title = SOCI Part 6A lawful disclosure register hygiene
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 4
```

Notable severity by `register_status`: `expired`, `unapproved`, `basis_missing` → high; `artefact_missing` → medium; `approaching_sunset` → informational.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a)** Positive control: author a test entry without lawful_basis; UC flags `basis_missing`.

**(b)** Negative control: complete a fully-approved entry; UC clears.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `SOCI — Lawful Disclosure Register`.

- Register entries by lawful_basis.
- Recipient organisation map.
- Sunset aging.
- Approver concentration.
- Cross-link to UC-22.52.24 (every authorised-disclosure detection is reconciled to a register entry).

**Runbook for `expired`** (owner: Information Security Manager, severity P2):

1. Within 14 business days: renew or remove the entry; downstream feed UC-22.52.24 must be refreshed.

**Runbook for `basis_missing`** (owner: Information Security Manager + General Counsel, severity P2):

1. Within 14 business days: capture lawful basis or revoke disclosure.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Downstream lookup drift**: ensure `protected_info_authorised_disclosure_lookup` is regenerated when the register changes.

**Confidential artefacts**: sealed orders are confidentially recorded; ensure the reference is sufficient.

**Product / vendor-specific failure modes**:

- **ServiceNow GRC — lawful_basis as free text**: ServiceNow GRC by default stores lawful_basis as free text, which breaks the controlled-vocabulary contract this UC depends on. Mitigation: use a Choice (drop-down) field in ServiceNow keyed on a controlled vocabulary of `s42_authorised_recipient_Secretary`, `s43_authorised_recipient_ASD`, `s44_authorised_recipient_court`, `s45_authorised_recipient_regulator`, plus an `other_basis_with_reference` option that requires an attached basis_artefact_url.
- **Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow — sn_grc.lawful_disclosure table not in default scope**: the default ServiceNow add-on scope does not include `sn_grc_lawful_disclosure*` tables. Mitigation: extend `inputs.conf` to add a dedicated stanza for the GRC table and re-run discovery in the add-on UI.
- **OneTrust — alternative compliance tooling**: where OneTrust replaces ServiceNow GRC, the disclosure register lives in `RegisterEntries.json` exports rather than a stream-write integration. Mitigation: schedule a 30-minute scripted-input that re-exports the OneTrust register and writes the lookup; manage the staleness budget in `register_status` accordingly.
- **Splunk ES 7.x — notable severity floor**: ES 7.x bumps `severity=informational` notables to `low` if the notable index has `severity_field=severity`. Mitigation: write `urgency` rather than `severity` on the notable to bypass the floor.

**SOCI-specific failure modes**:

- **Section 42-45 alignment**: ensure the lawful basis maps to a specific subsection of Part 6A.
- **Cross-reference to TI**: some Protected Information may also be classified under the Telecommunications (Interception and Access) Act 1979; ensure dual-classification compatibility.
- **MSSP receipt**: an MSSP receiving Protected as part of triage; pair with explicit consent clause in MSSP contract.
grandmaExplanation: Sometimes we MUST share Protected information with the Government or a Court — the law allows it. We keep a written register of every allowed sharing: who, with whom, why, when. The yes-list is fed into the system that watches for wrongful sharing, so the alarms only ring on the real bad cases.
